D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

PenTile RGBG Sharpening

a green-rich panel renders only half as many reds and blues — sharpening hides the loss but stains text with magenta and green

A PenTile RGBG matrix gives every pixel a full-resolution green subpixel but shares red and blue between neighbours — only half as many of each. Fine detail in red and blue is sampled coarsely, so black text on white blurs and picks up colour at its edges.

Switch from the RGB stripe to PenTile and the same magnified word develops green/magenta fringes. Subpixel sharpening raises acutance, but it overshoots rather than restoring lost detail — push it and the fringes only grow louder.
